title: dipterocarpus retusus subsp. macrocarpus
intro: Dipterocarpus retusus subsp. macrocarpus, also known as the Philippine hardwood or giant stinkwood tree, is a subspecies of Dipterocarpus retusus that is native to the tropical forests of the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Dipterocarpus retusus subsp. macrocarpus include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Dipterocarpus retusus subsp. macrocarpus can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to support the body's natural healing processes.
